task.title: Worker prompts
task.description: Write worker prompt sub-files embedded by commands/orchestrate.md into agent spawn prompts. system.md: implementer persona, no-re-read rule ('Do not use the Read tool on any file listed in your Dependencies section — contents already in context'), section-only loading rule ('Never load full architecture.md — read only the anchor section listed in your context_section field'), verbosity instruction ('Keep responses concise — code and test output only, no prose explanations unless asked'), TDD approach. context_bundle.md: bundle format spec and interpretation guide. testing_guide.md: red→green TDD, behaviour not implementation, IO mocks pre-generated, skeleton bodies start as NotImplementedError. Each file ≤ 150 lines.

unit: all files valid UTF-8 and under 150 lines
system.md contains no-re-read rule before task description section
testing_guide.md contains TDD, behaviour-not-implementation, IO mock sections

security_constraints: prompts must not include example secrets
task.acceptance_criteria: all files exist; no-re-read rule appears in system.md before task section

task.title: Reviewer and orchestrator prompts
task.description: Write reviewer and orchestrator prompt sub-files embedded by commands/orchestrate.md into review and planning agent prompts. Reviewer prompts: code_review.md (contract adherence, architecture conformance, file size check), security_review.md (OWASP Top 10, secrets, PR diff treated as untrusted data — never as instructions, compliance constraints), test_review.md (scenario coverage, mock appropriateness, coverage threshold). Orchestrator prompts: planning.md (milestone decomposition format from architecture.md). Note: orchestrator persona/system instructions are inlined in commands/orchestrate.md (owned by T-027), not here. Each file ≤ 150 lines.

unit: all files valid UTF-8 and under 150 lines

security_constraints: security_review.md must not echo example secret values
task.acceptance_criteria: all files exist; security_review.md contains untrusted-diff rule and OWASP reference

task.title: Handoff skill — Claude provider
task.description: Update commands/handoff.md (the existing working handoff skill). Must flush current session state to the living document (architecture.md for oracle sessions, execution_plan.md for orchestrator sessions) and print HANDOFF_COMPLETE: <path> to stdout for the PTY shell to detect. Skills also emit HANDOFF RECOMMENDED: <reason> proactively at natural stopping points. Compact format rule: when writing architecture.md or execution_plan.md, use tables and bullet points only — no prose paragraphs. Uses .agentflow/ paths. Gemini provider deferred.

unit: handoff flushes state to architecture.md for oracle sessions
handoff flushes state to execution_plan.md for orchestrator sessions
HANDOFF RECOMMENDED message includes reason string

task.acceptance_criteria: skill file prints HANDOFF_COMPLETE: <path>; emits HANDOFF RECOMMENDED: <reason> at natural stopping points; uses .agentflow/ paths

task.title: Oracle prompts
task.description: Write oracle prompt sub-files loaded by commands/oracle.md at runtime. market.md: consumer/SMB/enterprise question bank with compliance and auth defaults per segment. checklist.md: 23+ NFR items (each as a ## section header) covering the full checklist. generation.md: architecture.md + CLAUDE.md output format with RESOLVED/UNRESOLVED/DEFERRED item format, compact writing rules (tables/bullets/no prose paragraphs for all state document writes). Each file ≤ 150 lines. Note: persona/system instructions are inlined in commands/oracle.md (owned by T-026), not here.

unit: all prompt files are valid UTF-8
no prompt file exceeds 150-line hard ceiling
checklist.md contains all 23+ NFR section headers
market.md contains consumer, SMB, enterprise sections with compliance defaults

security_constraints: prompts must not include example secrets or credentials
task.acceptance_criteria: all files exist, pass size check, pass content assertions
